# aarchbio

> Native arm64 (aarch64) rebuilds of BioContainers bioinformatics tool images,
> for Apple Silicon Macs and AWS Graviton. 500+ tools, cosign-signed, public, no
> account required. Point an nf-core / Nextflow pipeline at `quay.io/aarchbio`
> and it runs native — no QEMU emulation, no `exec format error`.

aarchbio solves a publishing gap, not a porting problem: bioconda already ships
`linux-aarch64` packages for most bioinformatics tools, but the BioContainers
images that pipelines pull are almost all `linux/amd64`. aarchbio rebuilds them
natively for arm64 and publishes them signed.

## Use it

- Nextflow / nf-core: set `docker { registry = 'quay.io/aarchbio' }` in nextflow.config
- Direct pull: `docker pull quay.io/aarchbio/<tool>:<version>--<build>`
- Tags mirror BioContainers' `<version>--<build>` scheme.
- Works on Apple Silicon and AWS Graviton; multi-arch where the tool is noarch.

## Verify (don't trust)

Every image is cosign keyless-signed and logged in the Sigstore transparency log:
```
cosign verify quay.io/aarchbio/<tool>:<tag> \
  --certificate-identity-regexp 'github.com/playgroundlogic/aarchbio' \
  --certificate-oidc-issuer https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com
```

## Scope

In scope: bioinformatics tool containers (the BioContainers / bioconda layer).
Out of scope (already arm64 or owned elsewhere): distro base images (Docker
Official), language/framework packages like tensorflow (conda-forge), and vendor
ML containers. aarchbio does not compile from source.

## Key links

- Website: https://aarch.bio/
- Catalog (browse all images): https://quay.io/organization/aarchbio
- Source & docs: https://github.com/playgroundlogic/aarchbio
- Request a tool: https://github.com/playgroundlogic/aarchbio/issues/new?template=request-container.yml
- Design & decisions: https://github.com/playgroundlogic/aarchbio/blob/main/DESIGN.md
- Known gaps & why: https://github.com/playgroundlogic/aarchbio/blob/main/GAPS.md

## Facts

- 500+ tools published, native arm64, signed, public (as of 2026).
- Kept current by a daily reconciler that diffs against the latest bioconda release.
- Maintained by Playground Logic. Apache-2.0. Unofficial — not affiliated with or
  endorsed by BioContainers, bioconda, nf-core, Seqera, or AWS.
